heading: Declaration

headingEvidence.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (eval: document.title = 'Declaration - Import and export applications - GOV.UK', H1=1, h1.textContent = 'Declaration'); corroborated trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 and trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (both h1 = 'Declaration').

bodyCopyNote: Every string below is transcribed VERBATIM from the rendered DOM, including its typos and unbalanced quotes (see openQuestions). No string in this array is paraphrased, tidied or re-cased. Conditionality is expressed in `structure` and `conditionalCopy`, NOT inside these strings.
bodyCopy: By clicking on the check box at the bottom of the page you are declaring that you have read and understood, accept and agree to the terms and conditions of the declaration.
Submission of the form will not be allowed until all fields have been completed and the check box has been ticked.
For England and Wales
The Animal and Plant Health Agency (APHA) will undertake control verification procedures on plants, plant products and other objects entering into England and Wales.

The APHA is an executive agency of the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (DEFRA), which executes plant health policy in England and Wales under a Memorandum of Understanding with Defra and with the Welsh Government.
APHA Import Enforcement Policy Statement
The Plant Health and Seed Inspectorate (PHSI) and the Horticultural Marketing Inspectorate (HMI) are part of the APHA and follow the DEFRA enforcement policy (PDF).
For Scotland
For plants, plant products and other objects entering into Scotland or destined for a Control Point registered in Scotland will be subject to control verification procedures by SASA.
SASA is a Division of the Scottish Government.
Statement of terms and conditions for applications
For the purposes of the declaration "APHA" refers to the HMI and the PHSI.

No liability shall attach to the APHA/SASA for any delay in granting or failure to grant a finalised Common Health Entry Document (CHED), nor any delay in inspecting or failure to inspect, delayed or non-delivery of any document.
The APHA/SASA shall be entitled to rely upon the accuracy of all documentation and information supplied by the operator responsible, for the inspection or for the issuance of a finalised CHED.
No finalised CHED shall be used issued in respect of any plants, plant products or other objects except those to which it applies.

Inspections will be carried out and finalised CHEDs will be issued only on the understanding that no liability shall attach to the APHA/SASA in any circumstance in respect of any inspection carried out or the issue of or contents of any CHED and that the APHA/SASA accepts no responsibility for any resulting loss however caused.

The responsibility for charges incurred in such inspections must be borne by the operator responsible for the consignment. Including payment for official controls, as well as for re-dispatching consignments, quarantine or isolation of consignments, or costs of destruction and disposal where necessary.
The APHA/SASA will not issue a finalised CHED in respect of plants, plant products or other objects, which in their opinion is incorrect or inaccurate.
Imports enquiries
APHA:

Animal and Plant Health Agency,
Foss House
Kings Pool, 1-2 Peasholme Green,
York,
YO1 7PX
Tel: 0300 1000 313
Email: phsi-importers@apha.gov.uk
SASA:
Email: hort.marketing@gov.scot
Data Protection Statement

Defra is the data controller for personal data you give to the APHA/SASA or what is held about you. APHA/SASA use it in line with the Data Protection Act. For more information visit on APHA’s (PDF) Privacy statement and SASA’s Privacy statement.
Legal Declarations
"I/We hereby request APHA/SASA to arrange for the examination of the consignment(s) of plants, plant products and other objects listed above and for the issue of finalised CHEDs as prescribed by:
Regulation (EU) 2016/2031, (retained EU legislation)"
Regulation (EU) 2017/625, (retained EU legislation)"
Regulation (EU) 543/2011, (retained EU legislation)"
Regulation (EU) 1333/2011, (retained EU legislation)"
I/We have read and accept the general conditions relating to the examination of consignments for import.

bodyCopyEvidence.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (eval: document.querySelector('main').innerText) — all 32 strings above were diffed character-by-character against that output, including the curly apostrophes in 'APHA’s'/'SASA’s', the 'used issued' typo, and the four unbalanced closing quotes. Corroborated trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97. Template: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/declarationChedpp.html.
bodyCopyEvidence.stabilityNote: The word 'retained' in the four regulation bullets is HARDCODED in declarationChedpp.html:107-110, not templated. The route handler does compute a `regulation: isChedDOrChedP ? 'assimilated' : 'retained'` context value (routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:61-64) but the CHED-PP template never consumes it — that variable serves the CHED-D/CHED-P templates. So this copy is static for CHED-PP and safe to treat as fixed.

conditionalCopy.text: As my consignment is in scope of the common user charge, I confirm I have read and agree to comply with the common user charge payment terms and conditions.
conditionalCopy.renderedWhen: The notification is billable — template guard {{#if isBillable}}
conditionalCopy.note: Copy is CONFIRMED verbatim from rendered DOM. The RULE that decides billability is separately `inferred` — see conditionalCopyRule below. Adds body copy only: it introduces NO form control.
conditionalCopy.evidence: trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval on #cuc-declaration-text returned the full outerHTML; innerText matches the string above exactly). This is the only CUC trace located in the CHED-PP corpus — driven by tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-cuc.spec.ts:11 ('B2C notifier submits CUC CHEDPP → notification is NEW and SOAP returns the record'). Absent on the non-billable variant: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 and trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 both eval CUC=0.

conditionalCopyRule.claim: The CUC paragraph renders when ALL THREE of these hold: (1) the `enableCommonUserChargeChanges` feature flag is ON; (2) notification.type !== CVEDA (i.e. not CHED-A); (3) notification.partOne.isChargeable is truthy.
conditionalCopyRule.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/utils/common_user_charge_helper.js:8-13 — `const isBillable = (notification) => (featureFlagHelper.isFeatureEnabled(env.getFeatureFlag('enableCommonUserChargeChanges')) && notification.type !== CVEDA && notification.partOne?.isChargeable)`. Wired into the page at routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:79 (`isBillable: cucHelper.isBillable(notification)`) and consumed by the template guard at views/importer/declarationChedpp.html:117 (`{{#if isBillable}}`).
conditionalCopyRule.residualGap: What sets `partOne.isChargeable` is NOT decided by the frontend — it arrives on the notification from upstream. The CHED-P workflow comment ('CUC only — triggered by Sevington port') and the CUC test's use of a Sevington inspection premises suggest a BCP-driven rule, but that is the TEST exercising one known-chargeable route, not the rule itself. The upstream chargeability rule remains a `gap` for a human/policy owner.
conditionalCopyRule.rebuildNote: MATERIAL FOR THE REBUILD, and easy to miss: this paragraph is behind a FEATURE FLAG. A trace showing it is evidence of the flag-on behaviour only. Confirm with the team whether `enableCommonUserChargeChanges` is now permanently on in production before treating the CUC paragraph as unconditional-given-chargeable.

secondaryActionsEvidence.evidence: trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval over 'main button, main input[type=submit], main a.govuk-button' returned exactly ONE element: BUTTON 'Submit notification'). There are no secondary actions — no Save and return, no Cancel.

govukComponents.countScope: INSIDE <main> only. The document also contains one H2 outside main — see nonStandardPatterns note on heading order.

Identical on the billable variant (the CUC paragraph adds no heading).

govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (eval: 'BODY=16 CUC=0'); trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 (eval: 'BODY=16 CUC=0'); trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval: 'BODY=17 CUC=1'). The delta of exactly 1 proves the CUC paragraph is the only body-copy difference between the two variants.

govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (eval: 'LIST=2'); corroborated trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval: 'LIST=2')

govukComponents.usage: The single declaration-agree consent checkbox. Rendered WITHOUT a govuk-form-group, fieldset or legend — bare govuk-checkboxes div.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (locator resolved to <input value="yes" type="checkbox" form="declaration" id="declaration-agree" name="declaration-agree" class="govuk-checkboxes__input govuk-!-margin-top-3"/>; eval: 'FORMGROUP=0'); corroborated trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval: 'FORMGROUP=0' — confirms the absent form-group on the billable variant too). Template: declarationChedpp.html:132-140. QA: ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/DeclarationPage.ts:6-8 — getByRole('checkbox', { name: 'I/We have read and understood' }).

govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (eval on a.govuk-back-link returned the outerHTML above)

govukComponents.usage: Expected above the H1 when declaration-agree is missing. NOT observed in ANY trace, and NOT exercised by ANY test — every CHED-PP path ticks the checkbox before submitting, so the error state is never reached. Both sources of truth are silent on what it looks like.
govukComponents.evidence: Absence confirmed on all three rendered variants (trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76, trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84, trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 — eval 'ERRSUM=0'; all are valid happy paths). QA corroboration: every declaration call site checks the box first — ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:495, 736, 893, 992; tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:83; tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:180.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke class on the <form> wrapping the checkbox, hidden fields and submit button. Carries IPAFFS-specific styling only; no behavioural JS observed.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Cosmetic/legacy class with no Design System equivalent. Trivially droppable in the rebuild — the form needs no custom class at all.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — a plain <form> inside govuk-grid-column-two-thirds is sufficient.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: <input ... id="declaration-agree" form="declaration"> — checkbox uses the `form` attribute despite being a descendant of that same form
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The checkbox is physically inside <form id="declaration"> yet also carries form="declaration". Redundant.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Redundant markup, likely a leftover from a layout where the checkbox sat outside the form. Harmless but signals copy-paste drift. Do not replicate.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-checkboxes inside the form, no form attribute.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (locator resolved HTML shows form="declaration" on the input); template declarationChedpp.html:130 (form open) and :135 (the input)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: govuk-checkboxes used without govuk-form-group / govuk-fieldset / govuk-legend, with hand-rolled error injection
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A single consent checkbox rendered as a bare govuk-checkboxes div; the error span is injected by an {{errorSpan errors 'declaration-agree'}} helper and the error class by {{displayErrorClass errors 'declaration-agree'}} applied directly to the govuk-checkboxes div, rather than the standard govuk-form-group--error pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Departs from the Design System's Checkboxes markup contract — error styling and message placement are hand-rolled, so error states will not look or behave like standard govuk-frontend. The new app should use the standard form-group + error-message structure.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-form-group (+ govuk-form-group--error) wrapping govuk-checkboxes with a govuk-error-message, per the Checkboxes component.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: Template: declarationChedpp.html:132-140 (verified: `<div class="govuk-checkboxes {{displayErrorClass errors 'declaration-agree'}}">` then `{{errorSpan errors 'declaration-agree'}}`). Rendered DOM confirms the absence on BOTH variants — trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 and trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (eval: 'FORMGROUP=0').

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: H1 followed directly by H4 section headings (no H2 or H3 in the page content)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Six section headings within the declaration jump from h1 straight to h4, styled with govuk-heading-s.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Broken heading hierarchy — an accessibility defect (WCAG 1.3.1 / axe heading-order). Do NOT replicate; use h2 with govuk-heading-m/s in the rebuild. NOTE: the legacy a11y suite does NOT catch this — it restricts axe to the wcag2a/wcag2aa/wcag21a/wcag21aa tags, and axe classifies `heading-order` as a best-practice rule which carries none of those tags, so it is filtered out before assertion. The Declaration page therefore passes the legacy a11y test at 0 violations WHILE carrying this defect. A green legacy a11y run is NOT evidence the heading structure is sound.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 — a11y tree shows heading level=1 then level=4; heading-inventory eval returns H1 'Declaration' (inMain=true) followed directly by six H4s (all inMain=true), with H3=0. PRECISION NOTE: the document does contain ONE H2 — 'Support links', class govuk-visually-hidden, closest('main')===null — but it is footer boilerplate rendered AFTER the content, so it neither supplies the missing h2 level nor affects the h1→h4 skip. Tag-scope filtering: ipaffs-qa-automation/utils/accessibility-utils.ts:119 — new AxeBuilder({ page }).withTags(['wcag2a','wcag2aa','wcag21a','wcag21aa']).disableRules(['aria-allowed-attr']); default threshold maxViolations:0 / failOnViolations:true at accessibility-utils.ts:20-24, not overridden by tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: common/referenceNumberHeaderDiv — bespoke reference bar rendered OUTSIDE <main>
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the draft reference and type (e.g. 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990 - CHEDPP') in a div carrying only the govuk-!-padding-top-6 spacing utility, wrapping a <span class="heading-tertiary"> (a non-govuk class). It is a sibling of <main>, not a child. The partial is also passed status and version.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is doing the job of a GOV.UK section caption but is neither a caption component nor inside the main landmark. Being outside <main> means the 'Skip to content' link jumps PAST it, so the draft reference — the user's key orientation cue on the terminal page of the journey — is bypassed for keyboard and screen-reader users. It also has no semantic association with the H1. Note this partial is shared across the journey, so the finding is service-wide, not specific to Declaration.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: <span class="govuk-caption-xl"> inside main, immediately above the H1, so it is announced as part of the page heading block.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence:
trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 — a11y tree shows the div at ref e50 as a SIBLING of main (ref e51); eval on .govuk-\!-padding-top-6 returned '
DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990 - CHEDPP
', confirming both the sole utility class on the div and the non-govuk heading-tertiary span inside it. Template: declarationChedpp.html:9 (partial invoked before
opens).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: PDF link accessible names describe behaviour, not destination — and do not contain the visible text
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The two outbound PDF links have accessible names 'Opens an enforcement policy document in PDF format.' and "Opens an APHA's document in PDF format.", while their VISIBLE text is 'enforcement policy (PDF)' and 'APHA’s (PDF)' respectively.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The accessible name does not contain the visible label, which fails WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name (an AA criterion) and breaks speech input — a voice-control user saying 'click enforcement policy' will not match the link. Compounding this, the link only wraps part of its phrase, so the Data Protection paragraph reads 'visit on APHA’s (PDF) Privacy statement', which is not sensible link text in isolation. Like the heading-order defect, the legacy a11y suite does not catch this. Note the third link ('SASA’s' → sasa.gov.uk/content/privacy) has NO aria-label and so does not have this defect — the inconsistency is itself a smell.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No component needed — use visible link text that names the destination (e.g. 'DEFRA enforcement policy (PDF)', 'APHA privacy notice (PDF)') and drop the overriding aria-label. GOV.UK content guidance: front-load link text with the destination, and mark file type/size in the visible text.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 — link inventory eval over 'main a' returned exactly three links: TXT=[enforcement policy (PDF)] ARIA=[Opens an enforcement policy document in PDF format.] HREF=.../470454/defra-enforcement-policy-statement-2015.pdf; TXT=[APHA’s (PDF)] ARIA=[Opens an APHA's document in PDF format.] HREF=.../897608/apha-privacy-notice.pdf; TXT=[SASA’s] ARIA=[-] HREF=https://www.sasa.gov.uk/content/privacy.

fields.name: declaration-agree
fields.label: I/We have read and understood the Conditions, Data Protection Statement and Legal Declarations
fields.requiredEnforcement: Server-side only. The HTML `required` attribute is FALSE on the rendered input.
fields.requiredNote: LEGACY (authorised source): mandatory per the frontend Joi schema — `'declaration-agree': Joi.string().required()` at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:13. This is a UI-only submit gate: the value ('yes') is never persisted to PartOne, so it carries NO backend @NotNull group and there is no backend ValidationMessages entry for it. Its mandatoriness is a frontend consent policy the rebuild may revisit (e.g. move to a standard govuk-form-group + govuk-error-message pattern), but the requirement itself — you cannot submit without ticking it — is legacy-fixed.
fields.evidence: EXISTENCE + VALUE (confirmed): trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (locator resolved to <input value="yes" type="checkbox" form="declaration" id="declaration-agree" name="declaration-agree" class="govuk-checkboxes__input govuk-!-margin-top-3"/>; control-inventory eval: 'INPUT|checkbox|declaration-agree|id=declaration-agree|req=false|val=yes'); corroborated trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 and trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97. REQUIREDNESS (inferred, source-only — the error state is never rendered in any trace): Joi at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:13 — `'declaration-agree': Joi.string().required()`. QA corroboration: DeclarationPage.ts:6-8 exposes it as checkboxReadAndUnderstood and EVERY CHED-PP submit path checks it (ched-pp-workflows.ts:495, 736, 893, 992) — the tests treat it as unconditionally required, never submitting without it.

fields.name: submissionDate
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.requiredNote: SPLIT MANDATORINESS (legacy). The FRONTEND round-trip requires it — `submissionDate: Joi.any().required()` at declaration.js:17 — so the POST fails if the hidden field is stripped. But the BACKEND model does NOT: PartOne.java:612 declares `private LocalDateTime submissionDate;` with NO @NotNull group annotation (CHED-PP or otherwise), so the persisted schema treats submission date as optional. The frontend requiredness exists only to force the server-rendered value back on submit, not because the domain model mandates it. The rebuild should derive submission date server-side at submit time (see the tamperable-field openQuestion) — the frontend Joi.required() here is a legacy round-trip artefact, not a real business rule.
fields.evidence: EXISTENCE + VALUE (confirmed): trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (control inventory: 'INPUT|hidden|submissionDate|id=|req=false|val=16 July 2026'); corroborated trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 and trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 (same name, same '16 July 2026' format). REQUIREDNESS (inferred): `submissionDate: Joi.any().required()` at declaration.js:17. ORIGIN (inferred): server-rendered at routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:68 — `submissionDate: dateTimeUtils.formatDateForDisplay(notification.partOne.submissionDate || new Date())`, then posted back. Human-readable format 'D MMMM YYYY', not ISO. No page object or test touches it — it is invisible to the QA suite.

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: EXISTENCE + VALUE (confirmed): trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (control inventory: 'INPUT|hidden|etag|id=|req=false|val="0000000000BD2A09"'); a different value observed on trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 — confirms it is a per-draft varying token, not a constant. REQUIREDNESS (inferred): spread in via ETAG_SCHEMA at declaration.js:16 (from validation/common_schemas). Optimistic-concurrency token. No page object or test touches it.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: EXISTENCE (confirmed): trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76 (control inventory: 'INPUT|hidden|crumb|id=|req=false'); corroborated trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84 and trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97. Value is a CSRF token and is [REDACTED] here. REQUIREDNESS (inferred, and deliberately NOT cited to the Joi schema — VERIFIED ABSENT from it): the schema at declaration.js:12-18 contains ONLY declaration-agree, ETAG_SCHEMA and submissionDate; `crumb` appears nowhere in declaration.js. Its required-ness is enforced by the hapi `crumb` plugin at the framework level, outside this handler. Platform concern, not a business field — the rebuild's CSRF mechanism is an independent choice. No page object or test touches it.

fieldCompleteness.claim: The field list above is COMPLETE for both CHED-PP variants: exactly one visible control (declaration-agree) plus three hidden inputs (crumb, etag, submissionDate) and one submit button. The billable/CUC page adds body copy only — it adds NO controls.
fieldCompleteness.evidence: Independent control inventory (eval over 'main input, main select, main textarea') returns an IDENTICAL field set on all three traces: non-billable trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 76, non-billable trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 84, and billable trace a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec action 97 — each yielding exactly: declaration-agree (checkbox), crumb (hidden), etag (hidden), submissionDate (hidden). Button inventory returns exactly one BUTTON ('Submit notification'). The CUC branch of the journey (ched-pp-workflows.ts:480-488) collects billing address/email/telephone on EARLIER pages (Confirm billing details / Find an address / Billing select the address / Billing change contact details), never on the Declaration page. Corroborated against the template: declarationChedpp.html:132-144 contains exactly these four inputs and one button. Corroborated against source 2: DeclarationPage.ts:1-17 exposes only checkboxReadAndUnderstood (:6-8), checkboxIConfirm (:10-12, CHED-A only) and btnSubmitNotification (:14-16). The three hidden fields are invisible to the QA suite by construction (page objects only model role-based locators), so their absence from source 2 is NOT evidence against them — the traces and the template are the witnesses, and they agree.

validationMessages.message: You must confirm that you have read and understood the Conditions, Data Protection Statement and Legal Declarations
validationMessages.trigger: Submit notification pressed with the declaration checkbox unticked
validationMessages.evidence: LEGACY (authorised IPAFFS source, verbatim): ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:68 — `DECLARATION: 'You must confirm that you have read and understood the Conditions, Data Protection Statement and Legal Declarations',`. Selected for CHEDPP at validation/routes/handlers/importer/declaration.js:10 — `const declaration = (type === CHEDPP) ? DECLARATION : DECLARATION_CVEDA` — and bound to the ANY_REQUIRED error type at declaration.js:13-15. This is the SOLE authoritative source for the consent copy: the backend microservice ValidationMessages.properties has NO declaration-consent key (grep'd 'declar' → only GMS_DECLARATION / gmsdeclarationaccepted / CVEDA-decision keys, all for other pages), because the declaration checkbox is a frontend-only submit gate that is never persisted to PartOne, so there is no backend @NotNull message for it. NOT observed rendered in any trace and NOT asserted by any test. CORROBORATION ATTEMPTED AND FAILED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS: (a) no CHED-PP trace renders it — every CHED-PP path ticks the box first; (b) a full-text search of tests/, workflows/ and page-objects/ for 'read and understood' returns exactly ONE hit — the locator at DeclarationPage.ts:7 — and no assertion on the message. Confidence is `legacy` (read verbatim from the authorised frontend source) NOT `confirmed` (never rendered); the copy MUST still be re-confirmed with a content designer before the rebuild uses it verbatim.
validationMessages.renderingGap: The message TEXT is now legacy-sourced verbatim, but HOW it renders is a genuine gap: the template injects it via {{errorSpan}} into the govuk-checkboxes div (declarationChedpp.html:132-133), not via a standard govuk-error-message, and no trace shows the resulting markup or the error-summary title.

confidenceRationale: Page-level confidence stays `inferred` rather than `confirmed` for two reasons: (1) the validation error state is never rendered in any trace, so the error copy, error-summary title and inline error markup are source-derived only; (2) the CUC conditional-rendering RULE is source-derived and its upstream input (partOne.isChargeable) is unresolved. Everything else — the H1, all 32 body-copy strings, the conditional CUC paragraph's text, the complete field set across both variants, the link inventory, and the full component inventory — is `confirmed` from rendered DOM.
openQuestions: BILLABILITY — PARTIALLY RESOLVED, residual gap upstream. The frontend rule is now known (see conditionalCopyRule): the CUC paragraph renders iff the `enableCommonUserChargeChanges` feature flag is on AND type !== CVEDA AND notification.partOne.isChargeable (common_user_charge_helper.js:8-13 → declaration.js:79 → declarationChedpp.html:117). QUESTIONS FOR A HUMAN: (a) is `enableCommonUserChargeChanges` permanently on in production now, and should the rebuild treat the CUC paragraph as unconditional-given-chargeable? (b) What upstream logic sets `partOne.isChargeable`? The CUC test uses a Sevington inspection premises and the CHED-P workflow comments 'CUC only — triggered by Sevington port', which HINTS at a BCP-driven rule, but that is the test exercising one known-chargeable route, not the rule. The rebuild needs the actual chargeability rule from a policy owner — the trace corpus cannot supply it (only one CUC trace exists and it uses one BCP).

STILL A GAP — no trace and no test ever reaches the declaration validation error state. The error-summary copy, its title, the inline error-span text and the focus behaviour remain source-derived or unknown. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: what should the error message and error-summary title be in the rebuild? Do not assume the legacy string (en.js:68) is still the desired copy.

TEST-SUITE GAP WORTH FLAGGING — the QA suite has NO negative test for the declaration checkbox on any CHED type. The rebuild should add one (submit with the box unticked → error summary + inline error + focus management), since this is the single mandatory interaction on the terminal page of the journey.

DISAMBIGUATION — there are TWO declaration-ish pages in the CHED-PP journey and this spec covers only the terminal one (journey position 32). Distinct from this page there is a **GMS declaration** page much EARLIER in the journey — after Commodity extended description / total gross weight, before Transport details (ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:383-386). It is a different page object (page-objects/notification/GMSDeclarationPage.ts:1-13) with a different control type — a RADIO 'Yes, I confirm the above' (:6-8) and a 'Save and continue' button (:10-12), not a checkbox and not 'Submit notification'. It is CONDITIONAL and OFF BY DEFAULT: ChedPPNotificationConfig.gmsDeclaration is optional (ched-pp-workflows.ts:86-87) and DEFAULT_CONFIG sets it to 'No' (:159), so the workflow skips it unless a test opts in. Consequence: the GMS declaration page is almost certainly UNTRACED in our corpus and needs its own page spec — it must not be folded into this one, and its absence from the corpus is a coverage gap, not evidence it does not exist. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: what is GMS (General Marketing Standards?), what triggers the page in production, and is it in scope for the CHED-PP rebuild? confidence: inferred.

A SECOND ACTOR reaches this page and the trace corpus does not show it. A delegated-authority (DoA) AGENT can submit the declaration on behalf of another organisation: tests/auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:92-100 has 'Carol Clark (agent)' amend an existing notification, change the organisation, then drive the SAME two controls — agentPages.declaration.checkboxReadAndUnderstood.check() (:98) and agentPages.declaration.btnSubmitNotification.click() (:99). The Declaration page is NOT notifier-only; it is also the terminal page of an agent's amend-and-resubmit path, reached from Review notification via btnAmend rather than from a fresh journey. The page object is shared and the controls are identical, so there is no evidence of an actor-specific variant — but the legal copy ('I/We hereby request…') assumes the operator responsible. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: does the declaration wording/consent need to differ when a delegated agent submits, and is the agent or the represented organisation the legal signatory? confidence: inferred (no CHED-PP trace in our corpus is a DoA run).

COPY DEFECTS IN THE LEGAL TEXT — transcribed verbatim into bodyCopy above and deliberately NOT tidied, but they must NOT be carried into the rebuild unreviewed. (a) Unbalanced double quotes: the Legal Declarations paragraph OPENS a quote ('"I/We hereby request APHA/SASA...') which is then CLOSED four times over — each of the four regulation bullets ends 'Regulation (EU) …, (retained EU legislation)"'. (b) Probable typo: 'No finalised CHED shall be used issued in respect of…' (numbered term 3) reads as though 'used' or 'issued' should have been deleted. (c) 'For plants, plant products and other objects entering into Scotland … will be subject to' has no grammatical subject. These are policy/legal strings, so the fix is a content-designer + legal decision, NOT a developer tidy-up — flag them rather than silently correcting.

The declaration body is entirely CHED-PP/plant-health legal text (APHA, SASA, PHSI, HMI, four retained-EU regulations, two named privacy notices). It must be re-signed-off by the policy/legal owner for the rebuild rather than lifted from this spec: (a) confirm the retained-EU-legislation citations are still current; (b) confirm the APHA postal address, 'Tel: 0300 1000 313', phsi-importers@apha.gov.uk and hort.marketing@gov.scot are still correct; (c) both privacy-notice PDFs are pinned to assets.publishing.service.gov.uk attachment_data/file/ IDs (470454 from 2015, 897608 from 2020) — verify these have not rotted before reusing the URLs.

IPAFFS renders a SEPARATE template per CHED type (declarationChedpp.html vs declarationCheda.html) with entirely different legal copy and a different validation message (DECLARATION vs DECLARATION_CVEDA, en.js:68-69). CORROBORATED BY THE QA REPO: the shared DeclarationPage.ts carries TWO checkbox locators — checkboxReadAndUnderstood ('I/We have read and understood', CHED-PP/P/D) and checkboxIConfirm ('I confirm that I have', explicitly commented '// ched-a' at DeclarationPage.ts:9-12). The rebuild is CHED-PP only, so only the 'I/We have read and understood' checkbox and declarationChedpp.html's copy apply.

OBSERVATION, may matter for scope — the CHED-P and CHED-D suites click Submit notification WITHOUT ticking any checkbox, across FIVE call sites in THREE test files: ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:281; ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:202; ched-p-notification.spec.ts:221; ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:73; ched-d-manipulation.spec.ts:59 — every one calling declaration.btnSubmitNotification.click() with no preceding .check(). Contrast CHED-PP, where all six call sites tick first (ched-pp-workflows.ts:495/736/893/992, ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:83, ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:180) plus the DoA agent path (doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:98). These are passing tests, so if CHED-P/D had a mandatory checkbox they would fail — strong evidence the CHED-P/D declaration pages have NO mandatory consent checkbox. Out of scope for the CHED-PP rebuild, but flagged in case the team assumes all four CHED declarations behave alike: they demonstrably do not, and a shared declaration component would be the wrong abstraction. confidence: inferred.

`submissionDate` is posted as a human-readable string ('16 July 2026' in all three traces, all recorded on the same day) rather than ISO-8601 and is round-tripped through a hidden field. The handler derives it server-side (declaration.js:68) but then trusts the POSTed value back. The rebuild should derive submission date server-side at submit time rather than trusting a client-posted value — this is a tamperable field in the legacy design. Note the observed format is unconfirmed across month boundaries: all traces ran on 2026-07-16, so 'D MMMM YYYY' vs 'DD MMMM YYYY' zero-padding cannot be distinguished from this corpus.

BACK DESTINATION — inferred. The Declaration page is reached from the Review notification page: every workflow does reviewNotification.btnSaveAndContinue.click() immediately before the declaration (ched-pp-workflows.ts:492-495). So Back should return to Review notification / check-your-answers. Worth a human confirming the intended rebuild behaviour, since the legacy link is href="#" (JS history back) and has no real destination.

The H1→H4 heading jump is an accessibility defect the legacy a11y suite structurally cannot catch (axe is tag-restricted to WCAG A/AA at accessibility-utils.ts:119, and heading-order is an axe best-practice rule with no WCAG tag). Confirm the rebuild uses h2, that its a11y gate includes axe best-practice rules, and that content designers are happy to restructure the legal copy accordingly.
